name: QuasAr2

brightnessData.note: At 0.7 W/cm² and 800 W/cm² (comparable at both intensities)

QuasAr2 molecular brightness = 0.36× Archon1 (Piatkevich 2018 Fig. 2b, cultured neurons). Fig. 2b statistics (Suppl.): QuasAr2 mean = 519.07 (n=18), Archon1 mean = 1440.33 (n=23) → 519.07/1440.33 = 0.36. Reciprocal of the Archon1-page entry (2.78× QuasAr2 = 1440.33/519.07). Corrected from a prior value of 0.417, which was not consistent with the Fig. 2b means / the reciprocal 1/2.78.

description: Higher-sensitivity QuasAr variant spike counting and subthreshold event detection.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted means of the bi-exponential fit (Table S2, HEK293, n=6): activation (τup, -70→+30 mV) τ1 1.2 ms (68%), τ2 11.8 ms → τon = 0.68×1.2 + 0.32×11.8 = 4.6 ms; deactivation (τdown) τ1 1.0 ms (80%), τ2 15.9 ms → τoff = 0.80×1.0 + 0.20×15.9 = 4.0 ms. Previously stored the fast components (1.2 / 1.0 ms).

Amplitude-weighted means of the bi-exponential fit (Table S2, HEK293, n=7, 34°C): activation τ1 0.3 ms (62%), τ2 3.2 ms → τon = 0.62×0.3 + 0.38×3.2 = 1.4 ms; deactivation τ1 0.3 ms (73%), τ2 4.0 ms → τoff = 0.73×0.3 + 0.27×4.0 = 1.3 ms. Previously stored the fast components (0.3 / 0.3 ms).

Cross-GEVI measurement from the Archon paper (Piatkevich et al. 2018, Suppl. Table 1), QuasAr2 template re-measured in cultured neurons at 32 °C (n=11), 637 nm laser, 800 mW/mm², bi-exponential fit F(t)=A(C·exp(-t/τfast)+(1-C)·exp(-t/τslow)). On: τfast 0.9 ms (67%) + τslow 11.7 ms (33%) → 0.67×0.9 + 0.33×11.7 = 4.46 ms. Off: τfast 1.6 ms (76%) + τslow 20 ms (24%) → 0.76×1.6 + 0.24×20 = 6.02 ms. Slower than the Hochbaum 2014 values because measured in neurons at a different intensity/temperature. (Table cell annotates 34 °C, but the table footnote states on/off kinetics were measured at 32 °C.)

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F per 100 mV (-70→+30 mV), Table S2 / Fig. 1c. 1P widefield, 640 nm. The later 46% values (Piatkevich 2018, Villette 2019) were measured under different conditions.

sensitivityData.note: Zou et al. 2014 (eFRET/CAESR paper) Table 1 lists QuasAr2 ΔF/F for a single AP = 48% (cultured rat hippocampal neurons, 23 °C, direct retinal fluorescence, 640 nm, 200 W/cm²), reported side-by-side with their eFRET constructs, ArcLight and ASAP1. Corroborates the origin-paper value (Hochbaum 2014, 48% single-AP ΔF/F). Note the companion SNR=41 in the same Table 1 row is attributed in Zou's body text to Hochbaum (ref. 13, at 300 W/cm²), and the Table footnote's '(ref. 23)' annotation refers only to the QuasAr2 illumination intensity of 200 W/cm² (ref. 23 = Gong et al. MacQ paper), not to the ΔF/F value. Does not change the displayed median.

photostabilityData.note: QuasAr2 photobleaching, cultured neuron (Optopatch2), continuous 640 nm at 300 W/cm² (= 3000 mW/mm²) over 30 min (Suppl. Fig. 5A). % remaining after 1 min is approximate (read off the early phase of the exponential-fit trace, which shows ~1–6% loss in the first minute); the full digitized bleach curve (t75 ≈ 412 s) is stored in `photobleach` below. See also the main text: ~5% photobleaching typically observed over a 40-s acquisition at standard 300 W/cm² imaging.

spectrum.name: QuasAr2
spectrum.note: Excitation + emission digitized from the QuasAr2 (red) curve in Hochbaum et al. 2014 (Nat. Methods, doi:10.1038/nmeth.3000), Suppl. Fig. 2d — QuasAr2's own measured spectrum (excitation recorded at λem = 750 nm, emission at λexc = 600 nm, on solubilized protein). Both arrays normalized to max = 1.0; ex peak 590 nm, em peak 715 nm.

voltage.name: QuasAr2
voltage.note: Re-digitized 2026-07 from the red QuasAr2 fluorescence-vs-voltage trace in Fig. 1c (Hochbaum 2014), the same panel/method used for QuasAr1: F (a.u., normalized to 1.0 at −100 mV) color-masked and axis-calibrated (V −100 mV at the y-axis / +50 mV at the x-axis right end; F 1.0 at the x-axis / 2.5 at the y-axis top), then converted to ΔF/F relative to the −70 mV holding potential (= 0), overlay-verified against the figure. Positive-going, monotonic, saturating gently. The −70→+30 mV (100-mV) chord of this representative trace ≈ +84%, slightly below the n=5–9-cell mean of 90%/100 mV (Table S2) recorded in dynamicRangeData — the representative Fig. 1c trace runs a bit under the per-cell mean, as for QuasAr1. Replaces a prior fabricated linear reconstruction (8 evenly-scaled points hitting the 90% headline).

photobleach.note: Black SOLID QuasAr2 curve, 637 nm 2200 mW/mm²; ends ~53% at 900 s. In this panel the solid QuasAr2 trace is repeatedly OCCLUDED by the overlying colored Archon curves, so the stored curve is a smooth stretched-exponential fit through the reliable (un-occluded) segments rather than raw per-column points — shape/endpoints are reliable, the mid-curve is model-interpolated. Origin-paper photobleaching panel Fig. 2i, cultured mouse hippocampal neurons, continuous illumination. IMPORTANT: the five sensors in this panel were each imaged at a DIFFERENT intensity/wavelength (Ace2N-4aa-mNeon 13 mW/mm² 475 nm; QuasAr2 & Archer1 2200 mW/mm² 637 nm; Archon1 & Archon2 800 mW/mm² 637 nm), so the raw F/F0 cross-overlay (shared source+Fig. 2i) is NOT iso-condition — it reproduces exactly what the paper plotted. intensityMWmm2 is intentionally OMITTED (linear dose-scaling across a 13-2200 mW/mm² spread would give misleading values), so each t75 stands at its native intensity.

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neuron (Optopatch2), in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP width for QuasAr2. Hochbaum et al. 2014 Suppl. Fig. 5C directly plots the optically-recorded AP width vs illumination time measured at 30% and 50% recovery from peak fluorescence deviation (5-AP averages, 34 °C, 1 kHz frame rate, cultured neuron). AP50 (width at 50% recovery = FWHM-equivalent) is stable at ≈4.3–4.5 ms across the 30-min recording (blue trace); the stored 4.5 ms is that AP50 value read at the start of the recording. (AP30, the 30% level, ≈2.5 ms, red trace.) The single-AP waveform itself is in Fig. S5B / Fig. S6E; the main text separately reports that QuasAr2 broadens the optical AP by 650 ± 150 µs relative to the electrical waveform at 70% max depolarization. Value is kinetics/frame-rate-influenced (QuasAr2 τ ≈ 1.3–1.4 ms at 34 °C). Previously mis-cited as Fig. S6 (which is the F-V + voltage-step figure).
